title: Evidence Ledger (claims.json)
description: Deterministic, span-anchored extraction of every checkable claim in a paper. Produced by skills/evidence-ledger; consumed by every auditor skill. The ledger is the ONLY structure auditors are allowed to reason over — no auditor re-reads the raw PDF and invents structure. Every downstream finding must cite a claim_id from here.

paper_id.description: Stable id for the audited paper (arxiv id, slug, or sha).

observability_level.description: Evidence tier actually available. See references/observability-levels.md.

generated_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp (stamped by the caller; the extractor itself stays deterministic).

source_files.description: Every file the ledger was built from, with a content hash so findings are reproducible against an immutable input.

type.description: What kind of checkable thing this is.

text_span.description: VERBATIM source text (the exact sentence/phrase/cell). Findings quote this; no paraphrase.

section.description: abstract | intro | method | experiments | table:N | figure:N | appendix | ...

value.description: Parsed value, present for numeric/comparison claims. Lets the deterministic layer do arithmetic (delta checks, coherence) without an LLM.

refs.description: Cite keys / arXiv ids / DOIs referenced by this span (for citation claims).

evidence_anchor.description: sha256 of the source file (or table/artifact id) this span lives in — the immutable anchor a finding rehashes against.

confidence.description: Extractor's confidence the span was parsed correctly (low for OCR/PDF-text numbers).
